about.lead: I work on research systems, applied machine learning, and technical writing that keeps method and evaluation visible.
about.biographyMarkdown:
My interests sit between software systems and research practice. I am drawn to projects where technical work benefits from stronger continuity: environments that preserve context across reading and analysis, model pipelines that make assumptions and evaluation easier to inspect, and writing that records not only outcomes but the reasoning that produced them.

Much of my recent work has focused on research infrastructure, evidence-aware interfaces, and machine-learning studies in scientific and healthcare settings. Across those areas, I care about clarity, traceability, and the discipline required to keep results tied to source material, implementation details, and the limits of the underlying method.
about.currentFocusMarkdown:
## Current focus

- research workflows that connect literature review, evidence capture, and statistical analysis
- applied machine learning in medical and scientific settings, especially where evaluation matters as much as model performance
- technical writing that explains decisions, tradeoffs, and experimental results with precision
- systems that make it easier to revisit work later without losing the context in which it was done
